summary: Iteration 167 - Legislation Framework Update Verification Complete. All 29 pytest tests passed. Backend health check returns healthy. Login flow working after password reset. RECENT_LEGISLATION_UPDATES contains entries for nsw (6), vic (6), qld (2), sa (3), wa (2), tas (2), federal (2). All legislation injection tests verified: NSW homicide includes Jury Amendment Act 2024, NSW domestic_violence includes coercive control s.54D, QLD domestic_violence includes s.334C, NSW firearms_weapons includes Knife Crime Act, terrorism includes Federal Hate Crimes Act. System prompts include RECENT LEGISLATION AWARENESS and LEGISLATION ACCURACY anti-hallucination rules.
critical_code_review_comments: BACKEND: RECENT_LEGISLATION_UPDATES dict at line 1136 in offence_framework.py contains 23 total entries across 9 jurisdictions (nsw, vic, qld, sa, wa, tas, nt, act, federal)
BACKEND: _build_recent_legislation_context() in offence_helpers.py correctly filters legislation by state and offence category
BACKEND: get_offence_context() injects recent legislation block into LLM prompts
BACKEND: get_offence_system_prompt() includes RECENT LEGISLATION AWARENESS and LEGISLATION ACCURACY anti-hallucination rules
BACKEND: All 29 pytest tests in test_legislation_framework.py pass
FRONTEND: Login flow works correctly with email/password authentication
FRONTEND: Dashboard loads with case list showing 10 cases, 41 documents, 35 timeline events

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 167 verified the legislation framework update. Key changes: (1) RECENT_LEGISLATION_UPDATES dict added to offence_framework.py with 23 entries covering recent Acts from 2022-2025 including Coercive Control Acts (NSW s.54D, QLD s.334C, SA s.20A), Jury Amendment Act 2024 (NSW), Knife Crime Act 2024 (NSW), Crimes Legislation Amendment (Racial and Religious Hatred) Act 2025 (NSW), Criminal Code Amendment (Hate Crimes) Act 2025 (Cth), Youth Justice Act 2024 (Vic). (2) _build_recent_legislation_context() function in offence_helpers.py builds context blocks for LLM prompts. (3) get_offence_system_prompt() includes anti-hallucination guardrails (RECENT LEGISLATION AWARENESS, LEGISLATION ACCURACY). All 29 pytest tests pass. Note: Test user password was reset to 'Grubbygrub88' during testing.
